question_html: Write the hybridization, shape and magnetic character of [Fe(CN)6]4-.
question_text: Write the hybridization, shape and magnetic character of [Fe(CN) 6 ] 4- .
explanation: [Fe(CN) 6 ] 4- Oxidation state of Fe is +2. x + 6(-1) = -4 x - 6 = -4 x = -4 + 6 x = 2 Electronic configuration of Fe 2+ is [Ar]3d Being a strong field ligand cyanide CN - will cause pairing of electrons. {tex}\therefore{/tex} So, hybridisation of complex is d 2 sp 3 . The shape of complex will be octahedral as coordination number is 6. The compound is diamagnetic in nature as it do not have any unpaired electron.
solution: [Fe(CN) 6 ] 4- Oxidation state of Fe is +2. x + 6(-1) = -4 x - 6 = -4 x = -4 + 6 x = 2 Electronic configuration of Fe 2+ is [Ar]3d Being a strong field ligand cyanide CN - will cause pairing of electrons. {tex}\therefore{/tex} So, hybridisation of complex is d 2 sp 3 . The shape of complex will be octahedral as coordination number is 6. The compound is diamagnetic in nature as it do not have any unpaired electron.
solution_html:
[Fe(CN)6]4-
Oxidation state of Fe is +2.
x + 6(-1) = -4
x - 6 = -4
x = -4 + 6
x = 2
Electronic configuration of Fe2+ is [Ar]3d
Being a strong field ligand cyanide CN- will cause pairing of electrons.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 
So, hybridisation of complex is d2sp3.
The shape of complex will be octahedral as coordination number is 6.
The compound is diamagnetic in nature as it do not have any unpaired electron.

subject: Chemistry
question_html: What is meant by chelate effect? Give an example.
question_text: What is meant by chelate effect? Give an example.
explanation: The complexes are formed by chelating ligands like ethylene diamine (en), EDTA, etc. are more stable than those formed by monodentate ligands such as H 2 O or NH 3 . This enhanced stability of complexes containing chelating ligand is called chelate effect. The number of such ligating groups is called the enticing of the ligand. Example: [PtCl 2 (en)]
solution: The complexes are formed by chelating ligands like ethylene diamine (en), EDTA, etc. are more stable than those formed by monodentate ligands such as H 2 O or NH 3 . This enhanced stability of complexes containing chelating ligand is called chelate effect. The number of such ligating groups is called the enticing of the ligand. Example: [PtCl 2 (en)]
solution_html:
The complexes are formed by chelating ligands like ethylene diamine (en), EDTA, etc. are more stable than those formed by monodentate ligands such as H2O or NH3. This enhanced stability of complexes containing chelating ligand is called chelate effect.
The number of such ligating groups is called the enticing of the ligand.
Example: [PtCl2(en)]

  2. What type of isomerism is exhibited by the complex [Co(en)3]3+?

question_text: Write the IUPAC name of the complex [Cr(NH 3 ) 4 Cl 2 ]Cl. What type of isomerism is exhibited by the complex [Co(en) 3 ] 3+ ? Why is [NiCl 4 ] 2- paramagnetic but [Ni(CO) 4 ] is diamagnetic? (Atomic no. of Cr = 24, Co = 27, Ni = 28)
explanation: IUPAC name of the given complex [Cr(NH 3 ) 4 Cl 2 ]Cl is tetraamminedichlorido chromium(III) chloride. [Co(en) 3 ]Cl 3 : It exhibits optical isomerism and exists in two forms: dextro (rotate the plane polarized light towards right) and laevo (rotate the plane polarized light towards left) that can be represented as: In the complex [NiCl 4 ] 2- , Ni is in +2 oxidation state and the outer electronic configuration of Ni in +2 oxidation state is [Ar] 3d 8 4s 0 . The Cl - ion being a weak field ligand cannot pair up the two unpaired electrons present in 3d-orbitals. That means 3d-orbitals are not involved in hybridization. Thus, the complex is sp 3 -hybridised tetrahedral and is paramagnetic in nature due to the unpaired electrons. In the complex [Ni(CO) 4 ], the oxidation state of nickel is zero and outer electronic configuration of Ni is [Ar] 3d 8 4s 2 . In the presence of strong field ligand CO, the 4s-electrons shifts to the two half-filled 3d-orbitals and make all the electrons paired. The valence 4s and 4p-orbitals are involved in hybridisation i.e sp 3 hybridization Thus, the complex is tetrahedral but diamagnetic in nature due to the all paired electrons. .
solution: IUPAC name of the given complex [Cr(NH 3 ) 4 Cl 2 ]Cl is tetraamminedichlorido chromium(III) chloride. [Co(en) 3 ]Cl 3 : It exhibits optical isomerism and exists in two forms: dextro (rotate the plane polarized light towards right) and laevo (rotate the plane polarized light towards left) that can be represented as: In the complex [NiCl 4 ] 2- , Ni is in +2 oxidation state and the outer electronic configuration of Ni in +2 oxidation state is [Ar] 3d 8 4s 0 . The Cl - ion being a weak field ligand cannot pair up the two unpaired electrons present in 3d-orbitals. That means 3d-orbitals are not involved in hybridization. Thus, the complex is sp 3 -hybridised tetrahedral and is paramagnetic in nature due to the unpaired electrons. In the complex [Ni(CO) 4 ], the oxidation state of nickel is zero and outer electronic configuration of Ni is [Ar] 3d 8 4s 2 . In the presence of strong field ligand CO, the 4s-electrons shifts to the two half-filled 3d-orbitals and make all the electrons paired. The valence 4s and 4p-orbitals are involved in hybridisation i.e sp 3 hybridization Thus, the complex is tetrahedral but diamagnetic in nature due to the all paired electrons. .

  3. In the complex [NiCl4]2-, Ni is in +2 oxidation state and the outer electronic configuration of Ni in +2 oxidation state is [Ar] 3d8 4s0. The Cl- ion being a weak field ligand cannot pair up the two unpaired electrons present in 3d-orbitals. That means 3d-orbitals are not involved in hybridization. Thus, the complex is sp3-hybridised tetrahedral and is paramagnetic in nature due to the unpaired electrons.

    In the complex [Ni(CO)4], the oxidation state of nickel is zero and outer electronic configuration of Ni is [Ar] 3d8 4s2. In the presence of strong field ligand CO, the 4s-electrons shifts to the two half-filled 3d-orbitals and make all the electrons paired. The valence 4s and 4p-orbitals are involved in hybridisation i.e sp3 hybridization Thus, the complex is tetrahedral but diamagnetic in nature due to the all paired electrons.

question_html: Write the state of hybridisation, shape and IUPAC name of the complex [Co(NH3)6]3+(Atomic no. of Co = 27)
question_text: Write the state of hybridisation, shape and IUPAC name of the complex [Co(NH 3 ) 6 ] 3+ (Atomic no. of Co = 27)
explanation: In [Co(NH 3 ) 6 ] 3+ , Co exists as Co 3+ ion. The formation of [Co(NH 3 ) 6 ]3+ can be explained as Co-atom in ground state = Co 3+ ion = NH 3 being a strong field ligand causes pairing of 3d 6 electrons resulting in empty two 3d, one 4s and three 4p-orbitals which undergoes hybridisation to give six d 2 sp 3 -hybrid orbitals. These six empty d 2 sp 3 -hybrid orbitals would be accommodated by electron pairs of six NH 3 molecules resulting in the formation of the complex [Co(NH 3 )6] 3+ ion. Formation of [Co (NH 3 ) 6 ] 3+ ion It is inner orbital, low spin complex due to d 2 sp 3 -hybridisation. Thus, its shape is octahedral (as it is d 2 sp 3 -hybridised). IUPAC name is Hexaamminecobalt (III) ion.

question_text: For the complex, [Fe(CN)6] 3- , write the hybridisation type, magnetic character and spin nature of the complex. (Atomic number of Fe = 26) Draw one of the geometrical isomers of the complex [Pt(en) 2 Cl 2 ] 2+ which is optically active.
explanation: In [Fe(CN) 6 ] 3- complex, Fe is present as Fe 3+ . Configuration of Fe= [Ar] 3d 6 4s 2 4p 0 Outer configuration of Fe 3+ = 3d 5 CN - being strong field ligand, pair up the unpaired d-electrons. Thus, two 3d-orbitals are now available for CN - ions. {tex}\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_{6}\right]^{3-}{/tex} Since one electron remains unpaired, the complex is paramagnetic. Moreover, (n -1) d-orbitals are involved in bonding. So, it is an inner orbital or low spin complex. The complex, [Pt(en) 2 Cl 2 ] 2+ contains + two symmetrical bidentate ligands, ethylenediamine (en) and exists in two geometrical isomers, as and trans. Trans isomer being symmetrical does not show optical isomerism and hence, this isomer is optically inactive. While cis being unsymmetrical shows optical isomerism. Hence, the structure of geometrical isomer (i.e. trans-isomer) of the complex [Pt(en)iCl2]2+ which is optically inactive is as follows: trans [Pt(en) 2 Cl 2 ] 2+ isomer optically inactive (Superimposable mirror images)

subject: Chemistry
question_html:
What is meant by crystal field splitting energy? On the basis of crystal field theory, write the electronic configuration of d4 in terms of t2g and eg in an octahedral field when

  1. {tex}\Delta_{0}{/tex} > P
  2. {tex}\Delta_{0}{/tex} < P
question_text: What is meant by crystal field splitting energy? On the basis of crystal field theory, write the electronic configuration of d 4 in terms of t 2g and e g in an octahedral field when {tex}\Delta_{0}{/tex} > P {tex}\Delta_{0}{/tex} < P
explanation: When ligands approach a transition metal ion in a definite geometry, the degenerated-d-orbitals split into two sets, one with lower energy and the other with higher energy. The difference of energy between the two sets of d-orbitals is called crystal field splitting energy. It is denoted by {tex}\Delta_{0}{/tex} (for octahedral complexes) and {tex}\Delta_{t}{/tex} (for tetrahedral complexes). It is actually, measures the crystal field strength of the ligands. {tex}\Delta_{0}>P{/tex} , (pairing energy), the 4th electron pair up in one of the t 2g orbitals giving the configuration {tex}t_{2 g}^{4} e_{g}^{0}{/tex} . Ligands which produce this effect are known as strong field ligands and form low spin complexes. If {tex}\Delta_{o}<P{/tex} , the 4th electron enters one of the e g orbitals giving the configuration {tex}t_{2 g}^{3} e_{g}^{1}{/tex} . Ligands for which {tex}\Delta_{o}<P{/tex} are known as weak ligands and form high spin complexes.
solution: When ligands approach a transition metal ion in a definite geometry, the degenerated-d-orbitals split into two sets, one with lower energy and the other with higher energy. The difference of energy between the two sets of d-orbitals is called crystal field splitting energy. It is denoted by {tex}\Delta_{0}{/tex} (for octahedral complexes) and {tex}\Delta_{t}{/tex} (for tetrahedral complexes). It is actually, measures the crystal field strength of the ligands. {tex}\Delta_{0}>P{/tex} , (pairing energy), the 4th electron pair up in one of the t 2g orbitals giving the configuration {tex}t_{2 g}^{4} e_{g}^{0}{/tex} . Ligands which produce this effect are known as strong field ligands and form low spin complexes. If {tex}\Delta_{o}<P{/tex} , the 4th electron enters one of the e g orbitals giving the configuration {tex}t_{2 g}^{3} e_{g}^{1}{/tex} . Ligands for which {tex}\Delta_{o}<P{/tex} are known as weak ligands and form high spin complexes.
solution_html:
When ligands approach a transition metal ion in a definite geometry, the degenerated-d-orbitals split into two sets, one with lower energy and the other with higher energy. The difference of energy between the two sets of d-orbitals is called crystal field splitting energy. It is denoted by {tex}\Delta_{0}{/tex} (for octahedral complexes) and {tex}\Delta_{t}{/tex} (for tetrahedral complexes). It is actually, measures the crystal field strength of the ligands.

  1. {tex}\Delta_{0}>P{/tex}, (pairing energy), the 4th electron pair up in one of the t2g orbitals giving the configuration {tex}t_{2 g}^{4} e_{g}^{0}{/tex}. Ligands which produce this effect are known as strong field ligands and form low spin complexes.
  2. If {tex}\Delta_{o}<P{/tex}, the 4th electron enters one of the eg orbitals giving the configuration {tex}t_{2 g}^{3} e_{g}^{1}{/tex}. Ligands for which {tex}\Delta_{o}<P{/tex} are known as weak ligands and form high spin complexes.

subject: Chemistry
question_html: Explain the following: [Fe(CN)6]4- and [Fe(H2O)]2+ are of different colours in dilute solutions.
question_text: Explain the following: [Fe(CN) 6 ] 4- and [Fe(H 2 O)] 2+ are of different colours in dilute solutions.
explanation: In both the complexes, Fe is in +2 state with the configuration 3d 6 , i.e. it has four unpaired electrons. As H 2 O and CN - possess different crystal field splitting energy ( {tex}\Delta_{o}{/tex} ), they absorb different components of visible light (VIBGYOR) for d- d transition. Hence, the transmitted colours are different.
solution: In both the complexes, Fe is in +2 state with the configuration 3d 6 , i.e. it has four unpaired electrons. As H 2 O and CN - possess different crystal field splitting energy ( {tex}\Delta_{o}{/tex} ), they absorb different components of visible light (VIBGYOR) for d- d transition. Hence, the transmitted colours are different.

question_text: Name the following coordination entities and describe their structures : (Atomic no. of Fe = 26, Cr = 24, Ni = 28) [Fe(CN) 6 ] 4- [Cr(NH 3 ) 4 Cl 2 ] + [Ni(CN) 4 ] 2-
explanation: IUPAC name of the complex [Fe(CN) 6 ] 4- is hexacyanoferrate (II) ion. Structure of the given complex: It is an Octahedral complex due to d 2 sp 3 -hybridisation. IUPAC name of the complex [Cr(NH 3 ) 4 Cl 2 ] + is tetraamminedichloridochromium (III) ion. Structure of the given complex: It is an Octahedral complex due to d 2 sp 3 hybridisation. It is an inner orbital complex (as inner d-orbital is involve in hybridisation). IUPAC name of the given complex {tex}\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}{/tex} is tetracyanonickelate (II) ion. Structure of the complex: It is a square planar complex due to dsp 2 hybridisation.
solution: IUPAC name of the complex [Fe(CN) 6 ] 4- is hexacyanoferrate (II) ion. Structure of the given complex: It is an Octahedral complex due to d 2 sp 3 -hybridisation. IUPAC name of the complex [Cr(NH 3 ) 4 Cl 2 ] + is tetraamminedichloridochromium (III) ion. Structure of the given complex: It is an Octahedral complex due to d 2 sp 3 hybridisation. It is an inner orbital complex (as inner d-orbital is involve in hybridisation). IUPAC name of the given complex {tex}\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}{/tex} is tetracyanonickelate (II) ion. Structure of the complex: It is a square planar complex due to dsp 2 hybridisation.
solution_html:
1. IUPAC name of the complex [Fe(CN)6]4- is hexacyanoferrate (II) ion.
    Structure of the given complex: It is an Octahedral complex due to d2sp3-hybridisation.
  2. IUPAC name of the complex [Cr(NH3)4Cl2]+ is tetraamminedichloridochromium (III) ion.
    Structure of the given complex: It is an Octahedral complex due to d2sp3 hybridisation.
    It is an inner orbital complex (as inner d-orbital is involve in hybridisation).
  3. IUPAC name of the given complex {tex}\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}{/tex} is tetracyanonickelate (II) ion.
    Structure of the complex: It is a square planar complex due to dsp2 hybridisation.

subject: Chemistry
question_html:
State reason for each of the following:

  1. CO is stronger complexing reagent than NH3.
  2. The molecular shape of Ni(CO)4 is not the same as that of [Ni(CN)4]2-.
question_text: State reason for each of the following: CO is stronger complexing reagent than NH 3 . The molecular shape of Ni(CO) 4 is not the same as that of [Ni(CN) 4 ] 2- .
explanation: As CO is a good {tex}\sigma{/tex} -sigma donor and a good {tex}\pi{/tex} -acceptor ligand, there exists a back bonding in CO complexes in which CO accepts an appreciable amount of electron density from the filled d-orbitals of metal atom into their empty {tex}\pi{/tex} or {tex}\pi^*{/tex} orbitals. These {tex}\pi{/tex} interactions increase the value of {tex}\Delta_{0}{/tex} . Whereas NH 3 can form only {tex}\sigma{/tex} -bonds and no {tex}\pi{/tex} -bonds with metals, therefore CO is a better complexing reagent than NH 3 . In [Ni(CO) 4 ] -2 , Ni has an oxidation state equal to zero and involves sp 3 -hybridisation, hence possesses a tetrahedral shape. On the other hand, in [Ni(CN) 4 ] 2- , the oxidation state of Ni is +2 and it involves dsp 2 -hybridisation, hence, possesses a square planar structure.

In [Ni(CO) 4 ] -2 , Ni has an oxidation state equal to zero and involves sp 3 -hybridisation, hence possesses a tetrahedral shape. On the other hand, in [Ni(CN) 4 ] 2- , the oxidation state of Ni is +2 and it involves dsp 2 -hybridisation, hence, possesses a square planar structure.
solution_html:
1. As CO is a good {tex}\sigma{/tex}-sigma donor and a good {tex}\pi{/tex}-acceptor ligand, there exists a back bonding in CO complexes in which CO accepts an appreciable amount of electron density from the filled d-orbitals of metal atom into their empty {tex}\pi{/tex} or {tex}\pi^*{/tex} orbitals. These {tex}\pi{/tex} interactions increase the value of {tex}\Delta_{0}{/tex}. Whereas NH3 can form only {tex}\sigma{/tex}-bonds and no {tex}\pi{/tex}-bonds with metals, therefore CO is a better complexing reagent than NH3.
  2. In [Ni(CO)4]-2, Ni has an oxidation state equal to zero and involves sp3-hybridisation, hence possesses a tetrahedral shape.
    On the other hand, in [Ni(CN)4]2-, the oxidation state of Ni is +2 and it involves dsp2-hybridisation, hence, possesses a square planar structure.

  2. CO is a stronger complexing reagent than NH3.
question_text: Explain the following observations: CO 2+ is easily oxidised to Co 3+ in the presence of a strong ligand. CO is a stronger complexing reagent than NH 3 .
explanation: The electronic configuration of Co 3+ is 3d 6 4s 0 . So, pairing occurs in the presence of a strong ligand. Thus, there are no unpaired electrons and it is highly stable. {tex}\mathrm{Co}^{3+}{/tex} = 3d 6 , In the presence of a strong ligand, However, in Co 2+ , an electronic configuration is 3d 7 , there is one unpaired electron even after pairing occurs in the presence of a strong ligand. Co 2+ = 3d 7 , In the presence of a strong ligand This unpaired electron can be easily lost and shows an oxidation state of Hence, Co 2+ is oxidised to more stable Co 3+ . subject: Chemistry
question_html:
Give the formula of each of the following coordination entities:

  1. Co3+ ion is bound to one Cl-, one NH3 and two bidentate ethylene diamine (en) molecules.
  2. Ni2+ ion is bound to two water molecules and two oxalate ions. Write the name and magnetic behaviour of each of the above coordination entities (Atomic no. of Co = 27, Ni = 28).
question_text: Give the formula of each of the following coordination entities: Co 3+ ion is bound to one Cl - , one NH 3 and two bidentate ethylene diamine (en) molecules. Ni 2+ ion is bound to two water molecules and two oxalate ions. Write the name and magnetic behaviour of each of the above coordination entities (Atomic no. of Co = 27, Ni = 28).
explanation: The formula of the complex is [CoNH 3 Cl(en) 2 ] 3+ IUPAC name of the complex: ammine-bis-(ethane- I, 2-diamine) chloridocobalt(III) ion It undergoes d 2 sp 3 hybridisation. Since, the number of unpaired electrons = 0, so, the complex is diamagnetic in nature. The formula of the complex is {tex}\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{2}\left(\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}\right)_{2}\right]^{2-}{/tex} IUPAC name of the complex: diaqua dioxalato nickelate(II) ion Ni 2+ = 3d 8 4s 0 Number of unpaired electrons = 2 So, the complex is paramagnetic.
solution: The formula of the complex is [CoNH 3 Cl(en) 2 ] 3+ IUPAC name of the complex: ammine-bis-(ethane- I, 2-diamine) chloridocobalt(III) ion It undergoes d 2 sp 3 hybridisation. Since, the number of unpaired electrons = 0, so, the complex is diamagnetic in nature. The formula of the complex is {tex}\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{2}\left(\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}\right)_{2}\right]^{2-}{/tex} IUPAC name of the complex: diaqua dioxalato nickelate(II) ion Ni 2+ = 3d 8 4s 0 Number of unpaired electrons = 2 So, the complex is paramagnetic.
solution_html:
1. The formula of the complex is [CoNH3Cl(en)2]3+
    IUPAC name of the complex: ammine-bis-(ethane- I, 2-diamine) chloridocobalt(III) ion

    It undergoes d2sp3 hybridisation. Since, the number of unpaired electrons = 0, so, the complex is diamagnetic in nature.
  2. The formula of the complex is {tex}\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{2}\left(\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}\right)_{2}\right]^{2-}{/tex}
    IUPAC name of the complex: diaqua dioxalato nickelate(II) ion
    Ni2+ = 3d8 4s0

    Number of unpaired electrons = 2
    So, the complex is paramagnetic.

subject: Chemistry
question_html:
Give a suitable example for each, explain the following:

  1. Crystal field splitting.
  2. Linkage isomerism.
  3. Ambidentate ligand.
question_text: Give a suitable example for each, explain the following: Crystal field splitting. Linkage isomerism. Ambidentate ligand.
explanation: In most of the transition metal complexes, either six or four ligands surround the metal/ion. It gives rise to octahedral and tetrahedral structures. In both cases, the field produced by the ligands is not spherically symmetrical. As a result, d-orbitals are not equally affected by the field. It results in splitting the d-orbital energies. Linkage isomerism. It occurs in complexes when an ambidentate ligand occurs in the coordination sphere, e.g. [Co(NH 3 ) 5 NO 2 ] 2+ and [Co(NH 3 ) 5 (-DNO)] 2+ Another example is [Cr(H 2 O) 5 (SCN)] 2+ and [Cr(H 2 O)(NCS)] 2+ Ambidentate ligand: Ligands which can ligate through two different atoms present in it are called ambidentate ligands, e.g. NO 2 - , SCN -
solution: In most of the transition metal complexes, either six or four ligands surround the metal/ion. It gives rise to octahedral and tetrahedral structures. In both cases, the field produced by the ligands is not spherically symmetrical. As a result, d-orbitals are not equally affected by the field. It results in splitting the d-orbital energies. Linkage isomerism. It occurs in complexes when an ambidentate ligand occurs in the coordination sphere, e.g. [Co(NH 3 ) 5 NO 2 ] 2+ and [Co(NH 3 ) 5 (-DNO)] 2+ Another example is [Cr(H 2 O) 5 (SCN)] 2+ and [Cr(H 2 O)(NCS)] 2+ Ambidentate ligand: Ligands which can ligate through two different atoms present in it are called ambidentate ligands, e.g. NO 2 - , SCN -
solution_html:
1. In most of the transition metal complexes, either six or four ligands surround the metal/ion. It gives rise to octahedral and tetrahedral structures. In both cases, the field produced by the ligands is not spherically symmetrical. As a result, d-orbitals are not equally affected by the field. It results in splitting the d-orbital energies.
  2. Linkage isomerism. It occurs in complexes when an ambidentate ligand occurs in the coordination sphere,
    e.g. [Co(NH3)5NO2]2+ and [Co(NH3)5 (-DNO)]2+
    Another example is [Cr(H2O)5(SCN)]2+ and [Cr(H2O)(NCS)]2+
  3. Ambidentate ligand: Ligands which can ligate through two different atoms present in it are called ambidentate ligands, e.g.
    NO2- , SCN-

question_html:
For the complex [Fe(en)2Cl2]Cl, identify the following :

  1. Oxidation number of iron.
  2. Hybrid orbitals and shape of the complex.
  3. Magnetic behaviour of the complex.
  4. Number of its geometrical isomers.
  5. Whether there may be optical isomer also.
  6. Name of the complex.
question_text: For the complex [Fe(en) 2 Cl 2 ]Cl, identify the following : Oxidation number of iron. Hybrid orbitals and shape of the complex. Magnetic behaviour of the complex. Number of its geometrical isomers. Whether there may be optical isomer also. Name of the complex.
explanation: {tex}\left[\mathrm{Fe}(e n)_{2} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}\right] \mathrm{Cl}{/tex} x + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 0 + (-1) 2 = + 1 = + 3 Thus, the oxidation number of iron = + 3 In this complex, Fe is present as Fe 3+ and en being a strong field ligand, causes pairing. [Fe(en)Cl 2 ]Cl = d 2 sp 3 , octahedral geometry. Paramagnetic (as it contains one unpaired electron) Two geometrical isomers, cis and trans. Yes, cis isomer will have optical isomers, due to the non-superimposable mirror images. Dichlorido bis-(ethane I, 2-diamine) iron (III) chloride.
solution: {tex}\left[\mathrm{Fe}(e n)_{2} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}\right] \mathrm{Cl}{/tex} x + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 0 + (-1) 2 = + 1 = + 3 Thus, the oxidation number of iron = + 3 In this complex, Fe is present as Fe 3+ and en being a strong field ligand, causes pairing. [Fe(en)Cl 2 ]Cl = d 2 sp 3 , octahedral geometry. Paramagnetic (as it contains one unpaired electron) Two geometrical isomers, cis and trans. Yes, cis isomer will have optical isomers, due to the non-superimposable mirror images. Dichlorido bis-(ethane I, 2-diamine) iron (III) chloride.
solution_html:
1. {tex}\left[\mathrm{Fe}(e n)_{2} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}\right] \mathrm{Cl}{/tex} 
    x + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 0 + (-1) 2 = + 1 = + 3 Thus, the oxidation number of iron = + 3
  2. In this complex, Fe is present as Fe3+ and en being a strong field ligand, causes pairing. [Fe(en)Cl2]Cl = 

    d2sp3, octahedral geometry.
  3. Paramagnetic (as it contains one unpaired electron)
  4. Two geometrical isomers, cis and trans.
  5. Yes, cis isomer will have optical isomers, due to the non-superimposable mirror images.
  6. Dichlorido bis-(ethane I, 2-diamine) iron (III) chloride.

subject: Chemistry
question_html:
1. Write the IUPAC name and indicate the shape of the complex ion [Co(en)2Cl(ONO)]+.
    (Atomic no. of Co = 27)
  2. What is meant by chelate effect?
question_text: Write the IUPAC name and indicate the shape of the complex ion [Co(en) 2 Cl(ONO)] + . (Atomic no. of Co = 27) What is meant by chelate effect?
explanation: IUPAC name Chlorido bis-(ethylene diamine) The nitrito-O-cobalt (III) ion The complex is octahedral in shape as en is a bidente ligand, so coordination number = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 1 + 1 = 6. When a didentate or a polydentate ligand containing donor atoms positioned in such a way that, they coordinate with the central metal ion forming a five or six-membered ring the effect is called chelate effect. As a result of chelate effect, the stability of the complex increases.
solution: IUPAC name Chlorido bis-(ethylene diamine) The nitrito-O-cobalt (III) ion The complex is octahedral in shape as en is a bidente ligand, so coordination number = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 1 + 1 = 6. When a didentate or a polydentate ligand containing donor atoms positioned in such a way that, they coordinate with the central metal ion forming a five or six-membered ring the effect is called chelate effect. As a result of chelate effect, the stability of the complex increases.
solution_html:
1. IUPAC name
    Chlorido bis-(ethylene diamine)
    The nitrito-O-cobalt (III) ion The complex is octahedral in shape as en is a bidente ligand, so coordination number = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 1 + 1 = 6.
  2. When a didentate or a polydentate ligand containing donor atoms positioned in such a way that, they coordinate with the central metal ion forming a five or six-membered ring the effect is called chelate effect.
    As a result of chelate effect, the stability of the complex increases.

subject: Chemistry
question_html:
1. What do you understand by stepwise stability constant and overall stability constant of a coordination compound?
  2. How are these two constants related?
question_text: What do you understand by stepwise stability constant and overall stability constant of a coordination compound? How are these two constants related?
explanation: The equilibrium constant of each step of a complex reaction is called stepwise stability constant. Overall stability constant is the equilibrium constant for the net reaction. The stepwise stability constant can be given as M + L {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} ML; k 1 = [ML] /[M][L] ML + L {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} M L 2 ; k 2 = [ML 2 ]/[ML][L] ML 2 + L {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} ML 3 ; k 3 = [ML 3 ]/[ML 2 ] [L] ML 3 + L {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} ML 4 ; k 4 = [ML 4 ]/[ML 3 ] [L] where, k 1 , k 2 , etc. are referred to as stepwise stability constants. {tex}M+4 L \rightleftharpoons M L_{4} ; \beta_{4}=\left[M L_{4}\right] /[M][L]^{4}{/tex} where, P 4 is the overall stability constant. Thus, stepwise stability constants and overall constants are related as {tex}\beta_{4}=k_{1} \times k_{2} \times k_{3} \times k_{4} {/tex} or more generally, {tex}\beta_{n}=k_{1} \times k_{2} \times k_{3} \times k_{4} \dots k_{n}{/tex}
solution: The equilibrium constant of each step of a complex reaction is called stepwise stability constant. Overall stability constant is the equilibrium constant for the net reaction. The stepwise stability constant can be given as M + L {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} ML; k 1 = [ML] /[M][L] ML + L {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} M L 2 ; k 2 = [ML 2 ]/[ML][L] ML 2 + L {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} ML 3 ; k 3 = [ML 3 ]/[ML 2 ] [L] ML 3 + L {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} ML 4 ; k 4 = [ML 4 ]/[ML 3 ] [L] where, k 1 , k 2 , etc. are referred to as stepwise stability constants. {tex}M+4 L \rightleftharpoons M L_{4} ; \beta_{4}=\left[M L_{4}\right] /[M][L]^{4}{/tex} where, P 4 is the overall stability constant. Thus, stepwise stability constants and overall constants are related as {tex}\beta_{4}=k_{1} \times k_{2} \times k_{3} \times k_{4} {/tex} or more generally, {tex}\beta_{n}=k_{1} \times k_{2} \times k_{3} \times k_{4} \dots k_{n}{/tex}
solution_html:
The equilibrium constant of each step of a complex reaction is called stepwise stability constant. Overall stability constant is the equilibrium constant for the net reaction. The stepwise stability constant can be given as
M + L {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} ML; k1 = [ML] /[M][L]
ML + L{tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} M L2; k2 = [ML2]/[ML][L]
ML2 + L {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} ML3; k3 = [ML3]/[ML2] [L]
ML3 + L {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} ML4; k4 = [ML4]/[ML3] [L]
where, k1, k2, etc. are referred to as stepwise stability constants.
{tex}M+4 L \rightleftharpoons M L_{4} ; \beta_{4}=\left[M L_{4}\right] /[M][L]^{4}{/tex}
where, P4 is the overall stability constant. Thus, stepwise stability constants and overall constants are related as
{tex}\beta_{4}=k_{1} \times k_{2} \times k_{3} \times k_{4} {/tex} or more generally,
{tex}\beta_{n}=k_{1} \times k_{2} \times k_{3} \times k_{4} \dots k_{n}{/tex}

Among six d 2 sp 3 -hybrid orbitals, four orbitals occupied by electron pairs of four NH 3 molecules, and two are occupied by electron pairs of two Cl - -ions. explanation: [Co(NH 3 ) 5 Cl]Cl 2 IUPAC name Pentaamminechloridocobalt (III) chloride. Stereochemistry and magnetic behaviour [Co(NH 3 ) 5 Cl] 2+ = In [Co(NH 3 ) 5 Cl] 2+ , NH 3 being a strong field ligand causes pairing of electrons in d-orbitals of Co" ion. question_text: Using the valence bond theory predict the geometry and magnetic behaviour of [CoF 6 ] 3- . (Atomic number of Co = 27)
explanation: In {tex}\left[\mathrm{CoF}_{6}\right]^{3-}{/tex} , cobalt is in +3 oxidation state. F - is a weak field ligand. It does not cause pairing. In the formation of this complex outer six orbitals,i.e. one 4s, three 4p and two 4d-orbitals hybridise to give six hybrid orbitals (undergoes sp 3 d 2 -hybridisation). The complex has octahedral geometry and highly paramagnetic . It is an outer orbital or high spin complex.

subject: Chemistry
question_html:
Explain the following terms:

  1. Crystal field splitting in an octahedral field.
  2. Spectrochemical series.
question_text: Explain the following terms: Crystal field splitting in an octahedral field. Spectrochemical series.
explanation: Crystal field splitting In a free transition metal ion, all the five d-orbitals are degenerate (having equal energies). But when ligand approaches a metal ion, this degeneracy splits due to repulsion of electrons of ligands and electrons of metal ions. Fig., d-orbitals splitting in an octahedral crystal field Thus, the splitting of degenerate levels (or orbitals) due to the presence of ligands in a definite geometry is known as crystal field splitting in an octahedral complex. Spectrochemical series: The arrangement of ligands in the order of increasing field strengths, i.e. increasing crystal field splitting energy values is known as spectrochemical series. Br - < Cl - < F - H 2 O < NH 3 < en< CN - < CO

explanation: In case of Ni, electronic configuration of Ni= 3d 8 4s 2 and when it forms Ni 2+ ion it's electronic configuration becomes 3d 8 4s 0 . Ni 2+ = For low spin complexes, electrons get to pair up. Thus, will produce only one empty d-orbital. Hence, d 2 sp 3 -hybridisation is not possible in Ni to form octahedral complexes. The transition metals/ions have empty d-orbitals into which the electron pairs can be donated by ligands containing it-electrons (e.g. CH 2 = CH 2 and C 6 H 6 , etc.) while, the other metals do not have empty d-orbitals, hence, complexes are formed only by transition metal atom/ion.

subject: Chemistry
question_html: Square planar complexes with a coordination number 4 exhibits geometrical isomerism whereas tetrahedral complexes do not why?
question_text: Square planar complexes with a coordination number 4 exhibits geometrical isomerism whereas tetrahedral complexes do not why?
explanation: The tetrahedral complexes do not show geometrical isomerism because the relative positions of the atoms with respect to each other will be the same. The square planar complexes on the other hand show geometrical isomerism because if same kind of ligands occupy positions adjacent to each other it is called cis-form and if these are opposite to each other it is called trans-form.

question_html:
Explain the following:

  1. Low spin octahedral complexes of nickel are not known.
  2. The {tex}\pi{/tex}-complexes are known for transition elements only.
  3. CO is a stronger ligand than NH3 for many metals.
question_text: Explain the following: Low spin octahedral complexes of nickel are not known. The {tex}\pi{/tex} -complexes are known for transition elements only. CO is a stronger ligand than NH 3 for many metals.
explanation: In case of Ni, electronic configuration of Ni = 3d 8 4s 2 and when it forms Ni 2+ ion it's electronic configuration becomes 3d 8 4s 0 . Ni 2+ = For low spin complexes, electrons gets pair up. Thus, will produce only one empty d-orbital. Hence, d 2 sp 3 -hybridisation is not possible in Ni to form octahedral complexes. The transition metals/ions have empty d-orbitals into which the electron pairs can be donated by ligands containing it-electrons (e.g. CH 2 = CH 2 and C 6 H 6 , etc.) while, the other metals do not have empty d-orbitals, hence, complexes are formed only by transition metal atom/ion. As CO is a good o-sigma donor and a good {tex}\pi{/tex} -acceptor ligand, there exists a back bonding in CO complexes in which CO accepts an appreciable amount of electron density from the filled d-orbitals of a metal atom into their empty {tex}\pi{/tex} or {tex}\pi^*{/tex} orbitals. These {tex}\pi{/tex} interactions increase the value of {tex}\Delta_{0}{/tex} . Whereas NH 3 can form only o-bonds and no {tex}\pi{/tex} -bonds with metals, therefore CO is a better complexing reagent than NH 3 .

As CO is a good o-sigma donor and a good {tex}\pi{/tex}-acceptor ligand, there exists a back bonding in CO complexes in which CO accepts an appreciable amount of electron density from the filled d-orbitals of a metal atom into their empty {tex}\pi{/tex} or {tex}\pi^*{/tex} orbitals. subject: Chemistry
question_html:
Describe with an example of each, the role of coordination compounds in

  1. biological system
  2. analytical chemistry
  3. medicinal chemistry
question_text: Describe with an example of each, the role of coordination compounds in biological system analytical chemistry medicinal chemistry
explanation: Many biologically important natural compounds, exist as coordinate complexes e.g. chlorophyll, haemoglobin. Haemoglobin acts as O 2 carrier in the blood. Coordination compounds find use in many qualitative and quantitative chemical analysis. The familiar colour reactions are given by metal ions with a number of ligands, e.g. EDTA, DMG, {tex}\alpha{/tex} -nitroso- {tex}\beta{/tex} -naphthol, cupron, etc. involve the formation of coordination compounds. Many toxic metal ions are removed by the use of chelate therapy in medicinal chemistry, e.g. excess of copper and iron are removed by the chelating ligands such as D-penicillamine and des-ferrioximine Bvia formation of coordination compounds. EDTA is used in the treatment of lead poisoning. cis-platin is used in cancer therapy.

EDTA is used in the treatment of lead poisoning. cis-platin is used in cancer therapy.

  1. Linkage isomerism.
  2. An outer orbital complex.
  3. A bidentate ligand.
question_text: Explain the following giving an example in each case: Linkage isomerism. An outer orbital complex. A bidentate ligand.
explanation: [Co(NH 3 ) 5 (NO 2 )]Cl 2 and [Co(NH 3 ) 5 is(ONO)]Cl 2 are examples of linkage isomerism. Outer orbital complex When a complex entity uses outer orbital (nd) in hybridisation (sp 3 d 2 ), it is called outer orbital or high spin or spin-free complex. e.g [CoF 6 ] 3- complex is an outer orbital complex. Bidentate ligand When a ligand can bond through two donor atoms as in H 2 NCH 2 CH 2 NH 2 (ethane -1, 2 - diamine) or {tex}\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}^{2-}{/tex} (oxalate), the ligand is said to be bidentate.
solution: [Co(NH 3 ) 5 (NO 2 )]Cl 2 and [Co(NH 3 ) 5 is(ONO)]Cl 2 are examples of linkage isomerism. Outer orbital complex When a complex entity uses outer orbital (nd) in hybridisation (sp 3 d 2 ), it is called outer orbital or high spin or spin-free complex. e.g [CoF 6 ] 3- complex is an outer orbital complex. Bidentate ligand When a ligand can bond through two donor atoms as in H 2 NCH 2 CH 2 NH 2 (ethane -1, 2 - diamine) or {tex}\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}^{2-}{/tex} (oxalate), the ligand is said to be bidentate.
solution_html:
1. [Co(NH3)5(NO2)]Cl2 and [Co(NH3)5 is(ONO)]Cl2 are examples of linkage isomerism.
  2. Outer orbital complex When a complex entity uses outer orbital (nd) in hybridisation (sp3d2), it is called outer orbital or high spin or spin-free complex. e.g [CoF6]3- complex is an outer orbital complex.
  3. Bidentate ligand When a ligand can bond through two donor atoms as in H2NCH2CH2NH2 (ethane -1, 2 - diamine) or {tex}\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}^{2-}{/tex} (oxalate), the ligand is said to be bidentate.

subject: Chemistry
question_html: What are the shortcomings of valence bond theory for bonding in complexes? Briefly describe crystal field theory.
question_text: What are the shortcomings of valence bond theory for bonding in complexes? Briefly describe crystal field theory.
explanation: A number of assumptions are made. The theory does not explain the colour and spectra of complexes. It does not make exact predictions regarding tetrahedral and square planar structure of four coordinate complexes. It does not distinguish between weak and strong ligands. Crystal field theory: CFT to coordination compounds is based on the effect of different crystal fields (provided by the ligands taken as point charges) on the degeneracy of d-orbital energies of the central metal atom/ ion. The splitting of d-orbitals provides different electronic arrangements in strong and weak crystal fields.
solution: A number of assumptions are made. The theory does not explain the colour and spectra of complexes. It does not make exact predictions regarding tetrahedral and square planar structure of four coordinate complexes. It does not distinguish between weak and strong ligands. Crystal field theory: CFT to coordination compounds is based on the effect of different crystal fields (provided by the ligands taken as point charges) on the degeneracy of d-orbital energies of the central metal atom/ ion. The splitting of d-orbitals provides different electronic arrangements in strong and weak crystal fields.
solution_html:
1. A number of assumptions are made.
  2. The theory does not explain the colour and spectra of complexes.
  3. It does not make exact predictions regarding tetrahedral and square planar structure of four coordinate complexes.
  4. It does not distinguish between weak and strong ligands.

Crystal field theory: CFT to coordination compounds is based on the effect of different crystal fields (provided by the ligands taken as point charges) on the degeneracy of d-orbital energies of the central metal atom/ ion. The splitting of d-orbitals provides different electronic arrangements in strong and weak crystal fields.

  2. Explain as to how the two complexes of Ni,[Ni,(CN)4]2- and ​​​​​​​Ni(CO)4 have different structures but do not differ in their magnetic behaviour (Ni = 28).
question_text: What is a ligand? Give an example of bidentate ligand. Explain as to how the two complexes of Ni,[Ni,(CN) 4 ] 2- and ​​​​​​​Ni(CO) 4 have different structures but do not differ in their magnetic behaviour (Ni = 28).
explanation: The ions or molecules bound to the central atom/ion in the coordination entity, are called ligands. Ethane-1, 2, diamine (also called ethylene-1, 2 diamine) and abbreviated as -en is an example of bidentate ligand. {tex} {C_2}O_4^{2 - }{/tex} is another example. Ni in Ni(CN) 4 ] 2- is in +2 oxidation state while in Ni(CO) 4 it is in 0 oxidation state. [Ni(CN) 4 ] 2- [Ni(CN) 4 ] 2- Electronic configuration of Ni: [Ar]4s 3 3d 8 Electronic configuration Of Ni 2+ : [Ar]4s 0 3d 8 Outer electronic configuration of Ni 2- Pairing of electrons takes place in the presence of strong field ligand CN-. Thus, one 3d orbital becomes vacant. As the hybridisation involved is dsp 2 , we get square planar complex. As there are no unpaired electrons, the complex is diamagnetic. [Ni(CO) 4 ] Electronic configuration of Ni: [Ar]4s 2 d3 8 Outer electronic configuration of Ni(0): CO is also a strong field ligand. Pairing of electrons takes place. Electrons from the 45 orbital shift to 3d orbitals, Sp 3 hybridisation of orbitals takes place giving tetrahedral shape. ​​​​​​​ As there is no unpaired electron in this complex, it is diamagnetic. Thus, the two compounds do not differ in their magnetic behaviour.

question_text: What is the basis of formation of spectrochemical series? Draw the structures of geometrical isomers of the coordination complexes:[Co(NH 3 ) 3 Cl 3 and ​​​​​​​[CoCl 2 (en) 2 ] + .
explanation: It is an experimentally determined series based on the absorption of light by complexes with different ligands. Li general, the ligands can be arranged in a series in order of increasing field strength as given below: I - < Br < SCN - < Cl - < S 2- < F - < OH" < C 2 O < H 2 O < NCS - <EDT A 4- < NH 3 < en < CN - < CO Geometrical isomers of ​​​​​​​[Co(NH 3 ) 3 CI 3 ] Geometrical isomers of ​​​​​​​[CoCI 2 (en) 2 ] + ​​​​​​​

explanation: The IUPAC name of [CoCl (NO 2 )(NH 3 ) 4 ]Cl is Tetraamminechloridonitrito-N -cobalt (III) chloride.
